maths.question: What comes next? red, blue, red, blue, ?

english.prompt: Tap the words to make this sentence: I can run.
Tap the words to make this sentence: The cat sat.
Tap the words to make this sentence: We like jam.
Kofi has a red ball. He kicks it to Ama. They laugh.
The cat sat on the mat. It saw a bug. The cat ran.
Ama has a cup. The cup has milk. Ama drinks it.
Tap the words to make this sentence: Dad can hop.

science.prompt: What do plants need to grow?
science.fact: Plants use sun, water, and air to grow.

science.prompt: Which animal lays eggs?
science.fact: Many birds hatch from eggs.

science.prompt: What helps fish breathe in water?
science.fact: Fish use gills to take air from water.

science.prompt: Which one is a seed?
science.fact: A seed can grow into a new plant.

science.prompt: What do bees make?
science.fact: Bees help many flowers make fruit.

science.prompt: What pulls things down to the ground?
science.fact: Gravity helps keep our feet on the ground.

science.prompt: What gives us light in the day?
science.fact: Light lets our eyes see shapes and colours.

science.prompt: A ball rolls faster on a smooth floor or deep sand?
science.fact: Less rubbing can help things move faster.

science.prompt: What do magnets pull?
science.fact: Magnets pull iron and some other metals.

science.prompt: What can make a drum sound?
science.fact: Sound comes from tiny shakes called vibrations.

science.prompt: What is a map for?
science.fact: Maps show where places are.

science.prompt: Which place has lots of water?
science.fact: Oceans cover most of Earth.

science.prompt: Where do many crops grow?
science.fact: Farms help grow food for people.

science.prompt: Which is a hot dry place?
science.fact: Deserts get very little rain.

science.prompt: What country is Accra in?
science.fact: Accra is the capital city of Ghana.

science.prompt: Which shape has 3 sides?
science.fact: A triangle always has three sides.

science.prompt: Which shape is round?
science.fact: A circle has no corners.

science.prompt: How many sides does a square have?
science.fact: A square has four equal sides.

science.prompt: Which shape looks like a ball?
science.fact: A sphere is round like a ball.

science.prompt: Which shape can roll best?
science.fact: Round shapes roll because they have no corners.

science.prompt: Which star is closest to Earth?
science.fact: The sun is a star that warms Earth.

science.prompt: What do we breathe in?
science.fact: Air is all around us, even when we cannot see it.

science.prompt: What do clouds often bring?
science.fact: Rain helps plants, rivers, and farms.

science.prompt: Which body part helps you smell?
science.fact: Your nose can notice many different smells.

science.prompt: What changes ice into water?
science.fact: Heat can melt ice into water.

science.prompt: Who used a telescope to study the sky long ago?
science.fact: Galileo helped people learn more about space.

science.prompt: Who made a very useful light bulb better?
science.fact: Many inventors helped make electric lights useful.

science.prompt: Who studied tiny living things with a microscope?
science.fact: Microscopes help people see very tiny things.

science.prompt: What tool helped sailors find direction?
science.fact: A compass helps people know north, south, east, and west.

science.prompt: What did early farmers learn to grow?
science.fact: Farming helped people live in bigger communities.

science.prompt: Long ago, what did people use to write stories?
science.fact: People have shared stories for thousands of years.

science.prompt: Which old place has big pyramids?
science.fact: Ancient Egypt built huge pyramids long ago.

science.prompt: What did old traders carry between towns?
science.fact: Trade helped people share food, cloth, tools, and ideas.

science.prompt: What is a museum for?
science.fact: Museums help us remember people and places from long ago.

science.prompt: What helped people travel across the sea?
science.fact: Boats helped people visit far places.

coding.prompt: Daniel wants to wash, eat, then read. Which code is in the right order?
coding.hint: Do the steps from first to last.

coding.prompt: Noah builds a small boat. What should happen first?
coding.hint: A program starts with the first step.

coding.prompt: Ruth makes bread. Pick the code that makes sense.
Miriam follows a path to a basket. Which is a sequence?
coding.hint: A sequence has a beginning, middle, and end.

coding.prompt: The scroll code says: draw, color, show. What comes after draw?
The basket starts at the left. Which code moves it to the star?
Daniel is below the tent. Which command moves up?
Miriam needs to go right, then down. Which code matches?
coding.hint: Read the arrows from left to right.

coding.prompt: The sheep is one step left of the grass. Which command helps?
coding.hint: The grass is on the right.

coding.prompt: Which code makes a square path?
coding.hint: A square turns around four sides.

coding.prompt: Repeat clap 3 times. What does the loop do?
coding.hint: Repeat 3 means do it three times.

coding.prompt: The code says repeat 2: step. What happens?
Which code is shorter for star star star?
coding.hint: A loop can make three stars.

coding.prompt: Noah needs 4 taps. Which loop helps?
What does repeat 2: draw circle make?
Code says go right to the star, but the star is left. What is the bug?
coding.hint: The star is on the left.

coding.prompt: Miriam needs two steps, but code has one step. What fixes it?
coding.hint: The code needs one more step.

coding.prompt: The code says repeat 2, but we need 3 stars. What should change?
Daniel wants bread, but code picks a stone. What is the fix?
A bug makes the sheep go away from grass. Which command fixes it?
If it rains, then Ruth should pick what?
If the path has a wall, then what should the code do?
coding.hint: A wall means choose a new way.

coding.prompt: If the basket is full, then what should Miriam do?
If Daniel sees a star, then he should tap what?
coding.hint: The if rule names the star.

coding.prompt: If the light is red, then what should we do?

wordproblems.story: Kofi has 3 apples. Ama gives him 2 more.
wordproblems.question: How many apples now?

wordproblems.story: Abena has 5 balls. She gives 1 to Kwame.
wordproblems.question: How many balls are left?

wordproblems.story: Kwame sees 2 goats at the farm. He sees 3 more goats.
wordproblems.question: How many goats?

wordproblems.story: Ama buys 4 bananas at the market. Mum buys 4 more.
wordproblems.question: How many bananas?

wordproblems.story: Kofi has 6 books. He reads 2 books.
wordproblems.question: How many are not read?

wordproblems.story: Abena has 7 mangoes. She eats 2 mangoes.
wordproblems.question: How many mangoes left?

wordproblems.story: Kwame packs 5 pencils. Dad adds 3 pencils.
wordproblems.question: How many pencils?

wordproblems.story: Ama sees 4 hens. Two hens run away.
wordproblems.question: How many hens stay?

wordproblems.story: Kofi puts 2 cups on the table. Ama puts 5 more cups.
wordproblems.question: How many cups?

wordproblems.story: Abena has 8 shells. She gives 3 to Mum.
wordproblems.question: How many shells left?

wordproblems.story: Kwame has 1 red car and 4 blue cars.
wordproblems.question: How many cars?

wordproblems.story: Ama has 9 biscuits. She eats 4.
wordproblems.question: How many biscuits left?

wordproblems.story: Kofi sees 3 fish. Abena sees 6 fish.
wordproblems.question: How many fish together?

wordproblems.story: Kwame has 10 blocks. He drops 5 blocks.
wordproblems.question: How many blocks stay?

wordproblems.story: Ama plants 2 seeds. Kofi plants 2 seeds.
wordproblems.question: How many seeds?

wordproblems.story: Abena has 6 oranges. Grandma gives her 3 more.
wordproblems.question: How many oranges?

wordproblems.story: Kofi sees 5 stars. Then he sees 5 more stars.
wordproblems.question: How many stars?

wordproblems.story: Kwame has 4 drums. One drum is big.
wordproblems.question: How many drums?

wordproblems.story: Ama has 3 dolls. Abena brings 4 dolls.
wordproblems.question: How many dolls?

wordproblems.story: Kofi has 7 crayons. He loses 1 crayon.
wordproblems.question: How many crayons left?

vci.prompt: It has four legs and says woof. What is it?
What do we use to read?
It is yellow and you eat it. Monkeys like it.

fri.prompt: Which item is used for writing?

pa.prompt: cat, hat, dog, mat. Which one does not rhyme?
What sound does cat start with?
sun, fun, run, cup. Which one does not rhyme?
What sound does ball start with?

vsi.prompt: Which row is the same as ⭐🌙⭐?

passages.text: The cat sat on the mat.
I can see a big bus.
Dad has a red hat.
We go to school today.
The dog can run fast.
Ama has a cup.
Kofi can hop and run.
Mum can see the sun.
The hen is in the pen.
I like my red bag.
Kofi has a small bag. He puts a book in it. He goes to school.
Ama sees a hen at home. The hen sits by the pot.
Dad buys yam at the market. We eat it with stew.
The red bus is big. It stops near the school gate.
Mum has a cup of milk. The cat sits by her feet.
Abena has a ball. She kicks it to Kofi and they laugh.
The goat runs on the farm. Kwame sees it jump.
I see a fish in the pan. It is for lunch.
Grandma has a big basket. It has mangoes and nuts.
The sun is hot today. We sit in the shade.
Kofi and Ama went to the market with Mum. They saw red tomatoes, green peppers, and sweet bananas. Kofi counted ten oranges and helped Mum put them in the basket.
At school, OB read a small book about a dog. The dog ran after a ball and slept on a mat. OB smiled because he could read many words by himself.
Kwame visited the farm in the morning. He fed two goats and watched six hens peck corn. Then he told Dad how many animals he saw.
Abena made a card for Grandma. She used red, blue, and yellow crayons. Grandma hugged Abena and put the bright card beside her bed.
The family sat under a mango tree after lunch. Mum told a funny story, and everyone laughed. OB retold the best part in his own words.
Ama packed her school bag before bed. She put in a book, a pencil, and a snack. In the morning she was ready and calm.
Dad and Kofi counted cups on the table. There were four big cups and two small cups. Kofi said there were six cups altogether.
A little goat ran near the garden. It jumped over a log and made Ama giggle. She called Mum to come and see it.
The rain fell softly in the afternoon. OB looked out of the window and saw drops on the leaves. He drew the rain in his book.
At home, Grandma asked OB to sort beans. He made small piles and counted each one. Grandma said his careful counting was helpful.
